source.note: Unit 9 Grammar 1 (Cause and effect with double comparatives, planner pp.298-301) and Grammar 2 (Definitions with which, planner pp.304-305). No Grammar Workbook answer key was available for this unit -- charts use the general-purpose table shape built from planner content only.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Cause and effect with double comparatives
grammarPoints.rule: Use The more..., the more... or The more..., comparative adjective... to show that one action causes another.
grammarPoints.pattern: The more + subject + verb, the more + subject + verb  |  The more + subject + verb, comparative adjective + subject + verb

chart.title: Cause and effect with double comparatives

intro_examples.text: The more one skater pulls, the more the other one spins.
intro_examples.jp: 一方のスケーターが引けば引くほど、もう一方のスケーターはより回転します。

intro_examples.text: The more you push the swing, the faster it goes.
intro_examples.jp: ブランコを押せば押すほど、より速く動きます。

intro_examples.text: The more you practice, the faster you run.
intro_examples.jp: 練習すればするほど、より速く走れます。

rows.pattern: The more + subject + verb, the more + subject + verb
rows.example: The more one skater pulls, the more the other one spins.
rows.jp: 「the more」を二回使って、二つのことが一緒に増えることを表します。

rows.pattern: The more + subject + verb, comparative adjective + subject + verb
rows.example: The more you push the swing, the faster it goes.
rows.jp: 二つ目の部分で比較級の形容詞（fasterなど）を使うこともできます。

rows.pattern: The more + subject + verb, the faster + subject + verb
rows.example: The more you practice, the faster you run.
rows.jp: the fasterのように比較級を使って結果を伝えます。

rows.pattern: The more + subject + verb, the higher + subject + verb
rows.example: The more she pushes on the swing, the higher she goes.
rows.jp: the higherのように別の比較級を使うこともできます。

chart.note_rule: In English, we can use a double comparative to show that one action causes another: The more + subject + verb, the more + subject + verb, OR The more + subject + verb, comparative adjective + subject + verb.
chart.note_exception: A comma always separates the two halves of the sentence.
chart.note_exception_detail: One skater pulls. -> The other spins. cause -> effect. The more one skater pulls, the more the other skater spins.

table.title: Cause and effect with double comparatives

cells: the more the other one spins.
The more you push the swing,

notes: A comma separates the cause from the effect.
The first half always starts with The more.

tab1_samples.text: The more one skater pulls, the more the other one spins.
tab1_samples.jp: 一方のスケーターが引けば引くほど、もう一方のスケーターはより回転します。

tab1_samples.text: The more you push the swing, the faster it goes.
tab1_samples.jp: ブランコを押せば押すほど、より速く動きます。

tab1_samples.text: The more you practice, the faster you run.
tab1_samples.jp: 練習すればするほど、より速く走れます。

tab1_samples.text: The more he pushes down, the faster they go.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼が強く下に押せば押すほど、より速く進みます。

tab1_samples.text: The more she pushes on the swing, the higher she goes.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女がブランコを押せば押すほど、より高く上がります。

tab1_samples.text: The more the skateboarder pushes, the faster he moves forward.
tab1_samples.jp: スケートボーダーが押せば押すほど、より速く前に進みます。

tab1_samples.text: The higher she goes, the more she feels dizzy.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女が高く上がれば上がるほど、よりめまいを感じます。

tab1_samples.text: The more you play soccer, the better you get.
tab1_samples.jp: サッカーをすればするほど、より上手になります。

tab1_samples.text: The more we practice, the more we win.
tab1_samples.jp: 練習すればするほど、より多く勝ちます。

tab1_samples.text: The more force you use, the farther the ball goes.
tab1_samples.jp: 力を使えば使うほど、ボールはより遠くまで行きます。

rules.title: The more..., the more... shows two things increasing together
rules.jp_title: the more...、the more... は二つのことが一緒に増えることを表す
rules.subtitle: Use The more + subject + verb twice to show that as one thing increases, another increases too.
rules.jp_subtitle: The more + 主語 + 動詞 を二回使って、一方が増えるともう一方も増えることを表します。

transforms.from: One skater pulls a lot. The other skater spins a lot.
transforms.to: The more one skater pulls, the more the other skater spins.

transforms.from: We practice a lot. We win a lot.
transforms.to: The more we practice, the more we win.

examples.text: The more you study, the more you learn.
examples.jp: 勉強すればするほど、より多く学べます。

examples.text: The more it rains, the more the river rises.
examples.jp: 雨が降れば降るほど、川の水位はより上がります。

rules.title: Use a comparative adjective for a more precise effect
rules.jp_title: より正確な結果には比較級の形容詞を使う
rules.subtitle: Instead of repeating the more, use a comparative adjective (faster, higher, better) to describe the effect precisely.
rules.jp_subtitle: the more を繰り返す代わりに、比較級の形容詞（faster、higherなど）を使って結果をより正確に伝えられます。

transforms.from: You practice a lot. You run fast.
transforms.to: The more you practice, the faster you run.

transforms.from: You push hard. The ball moves far.
transforms.to: The harder you push, the farther the ball moves.

examples.text: The more you exercise, the stronger you get.
examples.jp: 運動すればするほど、より強くなります。

examples.text: The harder you push, the faster you go.
examples.jp: 強く押せば押すほど、より速く進みます。

rules.title: The comma always separates the two halves
rules.jp_title: コンマは常に二つの部分を分ける
rules.subtitle: Put a comma after the first The more clause, before the second half begins.
rules.jp_subtitle: 最初の The more の部分の後にコンマを置いてから、二つ目の部分を始めます。

transforms.from: The more you read the more you know.
transforms.to: The more you read, the more you know.

transforms.from: The more people recycle the cleaner the earth becomes.
transforms.to: The more people recycle, the cleaner the earth becomes.

examples.text: The more you smile, the happier you feel.
examples.jp: 笑えば笑うほど、より幸せを感じます。

examples.text: The more people recycle, the cleaner the earth becomes.
examples.jp: 人々がリサイクルすればするほど、地球はより清潔になります。

rules.title: Both halves need a subject and a verb
rules.jp_title: 両方の部分に主語と動詞が必要
rules.subtitle: Each half of the sentence needs its own subject and verb, just like two connected sentences.
rules.jp_subtitle: 文の両方の部分には、二つの文をつなげる時と同じように、それぞれ主語と動詞が必要です。

transforms.from: The more practice, the more win.
transforms.to: The more we practice, the more we win.

transforms.from: The more laugh, the more feel good.
transforms.to: The more you laugh, the more you feel good.

examples.text: The more friends you have, the more fun you get.
examples.jp: 友達が多ければ多いほど、より楽しくなります。

examples.text: The more you laugh, the better you feel.
examples.jp: 笑えば笑うほど、より気分が良くなります。

mixed_samples.text: The more one skater pulls, the more the other one spins.
mixed_samples.jp: 一方のスケーターが引けば引くほど、もう一方のスケーターはより回転します。

mixed_samples.text: The more you push the swing, the faster it goes.
mixed_samples.jp: ブランコを押せば押すほど、より速く動きます。

mixed_samples.text: The more you practice, the faster you run.
mixed_samples.jp: 練習すればするほど、より速く走れます。

mixed_samples.text: The more we practice, the more we win.
mixed_samples.jp: 練習すればするほど、より多く勝ちます。

mixed_samples.text: The more the skateboarder pushes, the faster he moves forward.
mixed_samples.jp: スケートボーダーが押せば押すほど、より速く前に進みます。

mixed_samples.text: The higher she goes, the more she feels dizzy.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼女が高く上がれば上がるほど、よりめまいを感じます。

mixed_samples.text: The more you play soccer, the better you get.
mixed_samples.jp: サッカーをすればするほど、より上手になります。

mixed_samples.text: The more you study, the more you learn.
mixed_samples.jp: 勉強すればするほど、より多く学べます。

mixed_samples.text: The harder you push, the faster you go.
mixed_samples.jp: 強く押せば押すほど、より速く進みます。

mixed_samples.text: The more people recycle, the cleaner the earth becomes.
mixed_samples.jp: 人々がリサイクルすればするほど、地球はより清潔になります。

explanation.title: the more..., the more...
explanation.body: Both halves use the more to show the two actions increasing together.

tab3_quiz.jp: 両方の部分に the more を使って、二つのことが一緒に増えることを表します。

stem: The more you push the swing,

explanation.title: the + comparative adjective
explanation.body: The second half uses the + comparative adjective (the faster) to describe the effect.

tab3_quiz.jp: 二つ目の部分では the + 比較級（the faster）を使います。

explanation.title: the faster
explanation.body: Use the faster, not just faster, to match the double comparative pattern.

tab3_quiz.jp: the faster を使い、faster だけにはしません。

stem: she pushes on the swing, the higher she goes.

explanation.title: The more starts the sentence
explanation.body: The first half of a double comparative always starts with The more.

tab3_quiz.jp: 文の最初の部分は必ず The more で始まります。

explanation.title: the faster
explanation.body: The second half needs the + comparative adjective: the faster.

tab3_quiz.jp: 二つ目の部分は the + 比較級が必要です：the faster。

explanation.title: the more
explanation.body: The second half uses the more to show the effect increasing.

tab3_quiz.jp: 二つ目の部分では the more を使って結果が増えることを表します。

explanation.title: the better
explanation.body: Good changes to the better in this pattern, not gooder or the goodest.

tab3_quiz.jp: good は the better に変わり、gooder や the goodest にはなりません。

stem: we practice, the more we win.

explanation.title: The more
explanation.body: Both halves of this sentence use The more / the more.

tab3_quiz.jp: この文の両方の部分に The more / the more を使います。

explanation.title: the farther
explanation.body: Far becomes the farther in this pattern.

tab3_quiz.jp: far は the farther に変わります。

explanation.title: the stronger
explanation.body: Strong becomes the stronger to match the pattern.

tab3_quiz.jp: strong は the stronger になります。

explanation.title: the more
explanation.body: Both halves need the more to show the cause and effect.

tab4_master.jp: 両方の部分に the more が必要です。

explanation.title: the faster
explanation.body: Use the + comparative adjective in the second half.

tab4_master.jp: 二つ目の部分では the + 比較級を使います。

tab4_master.cue: 練習すればするほど、より速く走れます。
tab4_master.jp: 練習すればするほど、より速く走れます。

tab4_master.cue: 一方のスケーターが引けば引くほど、もう一方のスケーターはより回転します。
tab4_master.jp: 一方のスケーターが引けば引くほど、もう一方のスケーターはより回転します。

explanation.title: the more
explanation.body: This sentence repeats the more in both halves.

tab4_master.jp: この文は両方の部分で the more を繰り返します。

tab4_master.cue: サッカーをすればするほど、より上手になります。
tab4_master.jp: サッカーをすればするほど、より上手になります。

explanation.title: the cleaner
explanation.body: Clean becomes the cleaner in this pattern.

tab4_master.jp: clean は the cleaner になります。

tab4_master.cue: 私たちが練習すればするほど、より多く勝ちます。
tab4_master.jp: 私たちが練習すればするほど、より多く勝ちます。

explanation.title: the farther
explanation.body: Far becomes the farther to match the pattern.

tab4_master.jp: far は the farther になります。

tab4_master.cue: ブランコを押せば押すほど、より速く動きます。
tab4_master.jp: ブランコを押せば押すほど、より速く動きます。

japanese.title: 二重比較級を使った原因と結果
japanese.rule: The more...、the more... または The more...、比較級... を使って、一つの行動が別の結果を引き起こすことを表します。
japanese.pattern: The more + 主語 + 動詞、the more + 主語 + 動詞 ／ The more + 主語 + 動詞、比較級の形容詞 + 主語 + 動詞

examples.sentence: The more one skater pulls, the more the other one spins.
The more you push the swing, the faster it goes.
The more you practice, the faster you run.
The more he pushes down, the faster they go.
The more she pushes on the swing, the higher she goes.
The more the skateboarder pushes, the faster he moves forward.
The higher she goes, the more she feels dizzy.
The more you play soccer, the better you get.
The more we practice, the more we win.
The more force you use, the farther the ball goes.
The more you study, the more you learn.
The more it rains, the more the river rises.
The more you exercise, the stronger you get.
The harder you push, the faster you go.
The more you smile, the happier you feel.
The more people recycle, the cleaner the earth becomes.

source.note: Grammar chart and practice sentences sourced from Level 4 planner LP pp.298-301 (Unit 9 Grammar 1); no Grammar Workbook answer key was available for this unit, so the chart uses the general-purpose table shape built from the planner content.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Definitions with which
grammarPoints.rule: Use which to give a definition or extra information about a thing (not a person).

chart.title: Definitions with which

intro_examples.text: Gravity is a force which pulls you toward Earth.
intro_examples.jp: 重力はあなたを地球の方に引く力です。

intro_examples.text: Skates are special shoes which you wear when you go ice skating.
intro_examples.jp: スケート靴はアイススケートをするときに履く特別な靴です。

rows.example: Gravity is a force which pulls you toward Earth.
rows.jp: which の後に動詞が続き、名詞について説明します。

rows.example: Skates are special shoes which you wear when you go ice skating.
rows.jp: which の後に主語と動詞が続くこともあります。

rows.pattern: This is a/an + noun + which + verb phrase
rows.example: This is a machine which you can ride in the park.
rows.jp: This is a/an ... which ... の形で定義を始められます。

chart.note_rule: Which introduces a clause that gives more information about a thing. It replaces it or them and comes at the start of the clause.
chart.note_exception: Which is used for things, not people.
chart.note_exception_detail: Skates are special shoes which you wear... (not who you wear — who is only used for people).

table.title: Definitions with which

cells: is a force which pulls you toward Earth.
are special shoes which you wear when you go ice skating.

notes: which is only used for things, not people (use who for people).

tab1_samples.text: Gravity is a force which pulls you toward Earth.
tab1_samples.jp: 重力はあなたを地球の方に引く力です。

tab1_samples.text: Skates are special shoes which you wear when you go ice skating.
tab1_samples.jp: スケート靴はアイススケートをするときに履く特別な靴です。

tab1_samples.text: A bicycle is a machine which you can ride in the park.
tab1_samples.jp: 自転車は公園で乗ることができる機械です。

tab1_samples.text: Friction is a force which happens when two things rub together.
tab1_samples.jp: 摩擦は二つのものがこすれ合うときに生じる力です。

tab1_samples.text: Tetherball is a game which two people play with a ball on a rope.
tab1_samples.jp: テザーボールはロープにつながれたボールで二人が遊ぶゲームです。

tab1_samples.text: A seesaw is a toy which goes up and down.
tab1_samples.jp: シーソーは上下に動く遊具です。

tab1_samples.text: A thaumatrope is a toy which uses two spinning pictures.
tab1_samples.jp: ソーマトロープは二つの回転する絵を使うおもちゃです。

tab1_samples.text: Soccer is a sport which many people play with a ball.
tab1_samples.jp: サッカーは多くの人がボールを使って行うスポーツです。

tab1_samples.text: A skateboard is a board which has wheels.
tab1_samples.jp: スケートボードは車輪がついた板です。

tab1_samples.text: A merry-go-round is a ride which spins around and around.
tab1_samples.jp: メリーゴーランドはぐるぐると回る乗り物です。

rules.title: Which replaces it or them
rules.jp_title: which は it や them の代わりになる
rules.subtitle: Combine two sentences by replacing it or them with which.
rules.jp_subtitle: it や them を which に置き換えて、二つの文を一つに結びつけます。

transforms.from: Gravity is a force. It pulls you toward Earth.
transforms.to: Gravity is a force which pulls you toward Earth.

transforms.from: Skates are special shoes. You wear them when you go ice skating.
transforms.to: Skates are special shoes which you wear when you go ice skating.

examples.text: A dictionary is a book which gives definitions of words.
examples.jp: 辞書は単語の定義を伝える本です。

examples.text: A gyroscope is a toy which spins in every direction.
examples.jp: ジャイロスコープはあらゆる方向に回転するおもちゃです。

rules.title: Use which for things, who for people
rules.jp_title: 物には which、人には who
rules.subtitle: Don't use which to describe a person — use who instead.
rules.jp_subtitle: 人を説明するときは which ではなく who を使います。

transforms.from: A skater is a person which moves on skates.
transforms.to: A skater is a person who moves on skates.

transforms.from: An athlete is someone which plays sports well.
transforms.to: An athlete is someone who plays sports well.

examples.text: A tool is an object which helps you do work.
examples.jp: 道具は作業を手伝ってくれる物です。

examples.text: A friend is a person who helps you.
examples.jp: 友達はあなたを助けてくれる人です。

rules.title: Start a definition with This is a/an...
rules.jp_title: This is a/an... で定義を始める
rules.subtitle: Use the frame This is a/an + noun + which... to define something clearly.
rules.jp_subtitle: This is a/an + 名詞 + which... の形を使うと、はっきりと定義できます。

transforms.to: This is a machine which you can ride in the park.
This is a game which you play by hitting a ball around a pole.

examples.text: This is a tool which measures direction.
examples.jp: これは方向を測る道具です。

examples.text: This is a place which has ice for skating.
examples.jp: これはスケートをするための氷がある場所です。

mixed_samples.text: Gravity is a force which pulls you toward Earth.

mixed_samples.text: Skates are special shoes which you wear when you go ice skating.

mixed_samples.text: A skater is a person who moves on skates.

mixed_samples.text: A gyroscope is a toy which spins in every direction.
mixed_samples.jp: ジャイロスコープはあらゆる方向に回転するおもちゃです。

mixed_samples.text: A thaumatrope is a toy which uses two spinning pictures.

mixed_samples.text: This is a game which you play by hitting a ball around a pole.

mixed_samples.text: An ice rink is a place which has ice for skating.

explanation.body: Gravity is a thing, not a person, so which is correct.

tab4_master.jp: 友達はあなたを助けてくれる人です。

japanese.title: which を使った定義
japanese.rule: which を使って、物についての定義や追加の情報を伝えます（人には使いません）。

examples.sentence: Gravity is a force which pulls you toward Earth.
Skates are special shoes which you wear when you go ice skating.
A bicycle is a machine which you can ride in the park.
Friction is a force which happens when two things rub together.
Tetherball is a game which two people play with a ball on a rope.
A seesaw is a toy which goes up and down.
A thaumatrope is a toy which uses two spinning pictures.
Soccer is a sport which many people play with a ball.
A skateboard is a board which has wheels.
A merry-go-round is a ride which spins around and around.
A dictionary is a book which gives definitions of words.
A skater is a person who moves on skates.
A gyroscope is a toy which spins in every direction.
This is a game which you play by hitting a ball around a pole.
An ice rink is a place which has ice for skating.

source.note: Grammar chart and practice sentences sourced from Level 4 planner LP pp.304-305 (Unit 9 Grammar 2); no Grammar Workbook answer key was available for this unit, so the chart uses the general-purpose table shape built from the planner content.
